View Patient Portal
Provider Dashboard — Brooke Welsh, WHNP

How It Works · Method & Compliance

HIPAA-eligible architecture, no EMR replacement, low-risk pilot path

156 Active Patients
5 Cohorts
Compliance & Architecture

No EMR replacement. HIPAA-eligible from day one. Pilot in weeks, not quarters.

Avail keeps the EMR they already use. We add a thin layer on top that turns consult recordings, lab data, and prior notes into draft protocols and cohort intelligence. Nothing is auto-sent. The provider always reviews and signs off.

The flow

1

Capture consult

Patient signs a one-page consent. Brooke records on her phone or laptop. Auto-uploads to a HIPAA-compliant intake.

→
2

Secure transcribe

Audio → text via a HIPAA-eligible transcription service (AWS Transcribe Medical, Azure Speech, or Whisper on a HIPAA-eligible host). PHI stays inside the BAA boundary.

→
3

Cohort match + draft

De-identified vector search across past patients. Claude (via AWS Bedrock w/ BAA) drafts the protocol with rationale and source citations.

→
4

Provider review

Brooke or Nicki reviews the draft, edits, approves. Only then does anything reach the patient. Sign-off is logged.

Compliance posture

HIPAA
  • BAAs signed with every vendor that touches PHI (AWS, Anthropic, transcription provider).
  • PHI stored only in HIPAA-eligible services (AWS HealthLake / S3 with encryption + audit).
  • De-identification before any cohort/analytics use (Safe Harbor method, 18 identifiers stripped).
  • Role-based access · audit logs on every read · session timeouts · MFA required.
  • Minimum-necessary principle: AI gets only the data it needs for the specific draft.
PCI
  • We don't touch payments. Avail keeps using their existing billing system.
  • PCI scope for this product = zero. If billing ever connects, it's via a tokenized handoff to a Level-1 PCI provider (Stripe, Square).
Consent
  • One-page recording consent added to existing intake. Patient can opt out anytime.
  • De-identified cohort data only — patients are never identifiable in trend views.
  • Pilot starts with 5–10 patients who explicitly opt in. Easy to expand once Brooke is comfortable.
Liability
  • The system drafts — it does not prescribe. Provider sign-off is mandatory and logged.
  • All sources cited — Brooke can see exactly which past patient or note influenced the draft.
  • Watch-fors flagged automatically (drug interactions, abnormal labs, contraindications).

Pilot path · low risk, fast to value

Phase 1 · Weeks 1–4

Manual workflow pilot

5 opted-in patients. Brooke records consult, uploads to intake form. Within 24 hours, AI returns draft protocol via secure email. No EMR integration. No live system. Just proof of value.

Investment: ~40 build hrs · ~$200/mo infra
Phase 2 · Weeks 5–12

Automated pipeline + cohort dashboard

Direct upload from phone. Real-time draft (under 60 sec). Patient persona dashboard live. Brooke can pull protocols by cohort.

Investment: ~120 build hrs · ~$400/mo infra (scales with usage)
Phase 3 · Optional

EMR read-only integration

If/when valuable: pull patient context from existing EMR via API (read-only, scoped). Still no EMR replacement. Brooke retains the system she already trusts.

Investment: scoped after phase 2 results

Tools we'd use (HIPAA-eligible)

Anthropic Claude via AWS Bedrock LLM with BAA · the engine that drafts protocols
AWS Transcribe Medical Medical-vocabulary speech-to-text · HIPAA-eligible
AWS S3 + KMS Encrypted storage · audit-logged · BAA
Pinecone (HIPAA-eligible tier) or pgvector De-identified embeddings for cohort matching
Existing EMR Stays put · no replacement · optional read-only API later
Existing billing Untouched · zero PCI scope
